articles.title: Heatwave School Holidays 2026: Delhi, Patna, Dehradun Revise Timings, Announce Closures - Dailyhunt
articles.search_term: heatwave (Almora OR Bageshwar OR Chamoli OR Champawat OR Dehradun OR Haridwar OR Nainital OR "Pauri Garhwal" OR Pithoragarh OR Rudraprayag OR "Tehri Garhwal" OR "Udham Singh Nagar" OR Uttarkashi) Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
A severe heatwave across India in 2026 has significantly disrupted the school calendar, forcing authorities to revise timings, declare early holidays, and even shut down institutions to safeguard students.
With temperatures soaring beyond 44°C in several regions, cities like Delhi, Patna, Dehradun, Noida, and parts of Rajasthan have implemented urgent measures to reduce heat exposure and ensure student safety.
Patna Schools Cut Short Timings
In Bihar's capital Patna, the district administration has ordered that schools for Classes 1 to 8 will operate only until 11:30 AM, effective from April 27. This step aims to protect young students from extreme afternoon heat, which can cause dehydration, heat exhaustion, and other health issues.
Dehradun Announces Complete School Closure
Authorities in Dehradun, Uttarakhand, have taken stricter action by closing all schools and Anganwadi centres due to the ongoing heatwave. This applies to all classes (1 to 12) across both government and private institutions.
Delhi-NCR: Revised Timings & Safety Measures
Although Delhi has not declared full closures, several precautionary steps have been introduced:
- Implementation of Heat Action Plan 2026
- Distribution of ORS (Oral Rehydration Solutions) to students
- Introduction of a "Water Bell" system to ensure regular hydration
- Restriction of outdoor activities between 11 AM - 4 PM
In nearby Noida, school timings have been revised to 7:30 AM - 12:30 PM for all boards, including CBSE and ICSE.
Other States Modify School Timings
To minimize exposure to peak heat, several states have shifted school hours to early mornings:

Due to rising temperatures, several states have also advanced their summer holidays:

articles.title: Schools shut in this Uttarakhand city amid intense heatwave; authorities declare multi-day holidays - Dailyhunt
articles.search_term: heatwave (Almora OR Bageshwar OR Chamoli OR Champawat OR Dehradun OR Haridwar OR Nainital OR "Pauri Garhwal" OR Pithoragarh OR Rudraprayag OR "Tehri Garhwal" OR "Udham Singh Nagar" OR Uttarkashi) Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
All schools in Dehradun have been closed today in view of the severe heat and the threat posed by the ongoing heatwave. A holiday has been declared today for all government, private, and non-governmental schools, as well as all Anganwadi centers.
Considering the continuously rising temperatures and the prevailing heatwave conditions in the Dehradun district, the district administration has taken a major decision today. As per the orders issued by the District Magistrate, all government, non-governmental, and private schools, along with Anganwadi centers across the district, are to remain closed today. This decision by the administration has been taken with the health and safety of the children in mind.
Schools Closed in Dehradun Today
The impact of the severe heat is clearly visible in Dehradun today. People are facing significant difficulties due to the scorching sun, rising temperatures, and hot winds. Following a warning issued by the Meteorological Department, the district administration has issued an order to keep all schools closed today as a precautionary measure. This decision has brought relief to thousands of students across the district today. Parents, too, feel less anxious regarding their children's health. The administration has clarified that this decision was taken specifically to protect children from health issues such as heatstroke and dehydration.
All Schools-Government and Private-to Remain Closed
According to the issued order, all government, non-governmental, and private schools in the Dehradun district-covering classes 1 through 12-will remain closed today. Additionally, a holiday has also been declared for all Anganwadi centers within the district today.
The Chief Education Officer and the concerned departments have been instructed to ensure strict compliance with this order. All school managements had already been asked to provide prior notification regarding this closure.
Decision Taken with Children's Health in Mind
Severe heat tends to have the most profound impact on young children. Exposure to the scorching sun and hot winds can lead to health issues in children, such as weakness, dizziness, dehydration, and heatstroke. Keeping these risks in mind, the administration has taken the decision to keep schools closed today.
Medical professionals emphasize that it is crucial to protect children from direct sunlight during this weather. Ensuring adequate hydration and staying safely indoors are the most effective measures.
The district administration has appealed to the public to avoid venturing outdoors during the afternoon hours. Special care should be taken of children, the elderly, and those in poor health. People are advised to wear light clothing and consume plenty of water. One should step out only when absolutely necessary and ensure their head is covered when exposed to sunlight. The administration is continuously monitoring the weather situation.
Further Major Decisions May Be Taken
If temperatures continue to rise in this manner over the coming days, the administration may take further significant decisions regarding schools. Currently, an order mandating the closure of all schools for today remains in effect.
